philippines.title: UAAP men's volleyball: FEU claims twice-to-beat, NU nears bonus
philippines.description:
MANILA, Philippines — Far Eastern University sealed a twice-to-beat advantage in the UAAP Season 88 men’s volleyball tournament after sweeping the also-ran University of the Philippines, 25-23, 25-18, 25-17, on Sunday at the UST Quadricentennial Pavilion. The Tamaraws had to overcome a 16-20 deficit, with Charles Absin, Dryx Saavedra, Amet Bituin, and Mikko Espartero leading the turnaround with a 9-3 run to draw the first blood and never looked back in the last two frames to rise to an 11-1 record. READ: UAAP: FEU nears twice-to-beat, defeats La Salle in men’s volleyball “We just hope what we’re doing now continues […]...
Keep on reading: UAAP men's volleyball: FEU claims twice-to-beat, NU nears bonus

philippines.title: MPD suspends ‘no shirt’ arrests amid ‘anti-poor’ concerns
philippines.description:
MANILA, Philippines—The Manila Police District (MPD) ordered the suspension of the enforcement of local ordinances penalizing shirtless individuals in public, which was deemed by some quarters as “anti-poor.” “Instead, they shall issue appropriate warnings and conduct information drives emphasizing proper decorum and community standards,” MPD spokesperson Maj. Philipp Ines said In a text message to reporters on Sunday. “In this regard, effective immediately, all operating units and personnel involved in Oplan Galugad [or search operations] and other anti-criminality operations shall refrain from taking punitive actions,” Ines said.He said the move seeks to maintain public trust and promote humane, compassionate policing […]...
Keep on reading: MPD suspends ‘no shirt’ arrests amid ‘anti-poor’ concerns

energy.title: How Iran’s Dark Fleet Is Quietly Keeping Oil Markets Afloat
energy.description: It almost looks like an eternal story or an Australian boomerang approach, but the global oil market is once again being misread, very badly. Headlines speak of disruption, paralysis, and the near closure of the Strait of Hormuz. International tanker trackers all report traffic collapsing, while Gulf exporters are shutting in production. Every single visible metric indicates that the system is under extreme stress. And yet, there is still oil flowing. This time it is not open, not as usual able to be measured by markets, or in volumes that are…

energy.title: Three Energy Stories That Actually Matter Right Now
energy.description: If you are looking for respite from news of bombings, threats to ancient civilizations, becalmed oil tankers, rising prices, disputes about who can go to which toilets, and sex scandals among the rich and famous, consider these stories that matter for energy folk who would have otherwise noticed but might have been distracted by all the other stuff.
